text: The surgeon performs a partial vaginectomy to excise a three-centimeter rectovaginal nodule. The rectovaginal space is developed to mobilize the rectum below the nodule's lowest margin. The nodule is tagged with a xeropolysorb suture for localization. Using blunt graspers, the margins of the nodule are delineated, revealing involvement only in the posterior vaginal wall. An inverted V-shaped incision is made with monopolar shears, and the nodule is transected using an anzeal bipolar device. Traction and counter-traction techniques ensure complete excision. The vaginal cuff closure begins with a unidirectional barb suture at the incision apex, re-approximating the posterior vaginal wall longitudinally. The rest of the cuff is closed transversely, with modified Richardson stitches placed at the vaginal cuff angles to incorporate the utero-sacral ligaments.

The surgeon performs a partial vaginectomy to excise a five-centimeter rectovaginal nodule located in the left cul-de-sac. The uterus is suspended to the anterior abdominal wall using a temporary suture and sterile IV tubing to optimize exposure. A temporary left oophorepexy is performed to access the left parametrium. Ureterolysis is conducted due to extensive endometriosis in the left ovarian fossa, with the left uterine artery and its vaginal branches temporarily ligated using endoclips. The rectovaginal space is developed, and the rectum is mobilized off the nodule and posterior vagina using sharp and blunt dissection. A Bryski-Navratol retractor is placed in the posterior vagina, and a colpotomy is initiated with monopolar shears. The nodule is nucleated from its attachment using a bipolar device. The colpotomy incision is reconstructed and reattached to the posterior cervix, with a two-layer closure using a unidirectional barbed suture. Endoclips are removed, and hemostasis is confirmed.
